TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua ka tjaisangas tangapuɫ i Vutsuɫ, saka keman tua tsautsau sakamaya palalaut.
S.PHON: izua ka caisaŋas taŋapuʎ i vuʦuʎ, saka kəman tua ʦauʦau sakamaja paɭaɭaut.
S.FORM: izua ka tjaisangas tangapulj i Vuculj, saka keman tua caucau sakamaya palalaut.
S.PHON: izua ka ʦaisaŋas taŋapuʎ i vuʦuʎ, saka kəman tua ʦauʦau sakamaja paɭaɭaut.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a lion in the Vutsuɫ area, and it kept on eating people.

S.FORM: saka "aku tjen a uri maquang?
S.PHON: saka "aku cən a uri maquaŋ
S.FORM: saka "aku tjen a uri maquang?
S.PHON: saka "aku ʦən a uri maquaŋ
S.TRANSL: They said: “We are going to be wiped out, aren’t we?

S.FORM: uri tja kudain sa tja sualapen a tangapuɫ?"
S.PHON: uri ca kudain sa ca suaɭapən a taŋapuʎ"
S.FORM: uri tja kudain sa tja sualapen a tangapulj?"
S.PHON: uri ʦa kudain sa ʦa suaɭapən a taŋapuʎ"
S.TRANSL: What are we going to do to get rid of this lion?”

S.FORM: qau djemauɫ tjai Tail kati Piri.
S.PHON: qau ɉəmauʎ cai taiɭ kati piri.
S.FORM: qau djemaulj tjai Tail kati Piri.
S.PHON: qau ɉəmauʎ ʦai taiɭ kati piri.
S.TRANSL: So they summoned Tail and Piri.

S.FORM: "keɫu, selapay anga men; uri maquang amen tusa tangapuɫ."
S.PHON: "kəʎu, səɭapaj aŋa mən; uri maquaŋ amən tusa taŋapuʎ."
S.FORM: "kelju, selapay anga men; uri maquang amen tusa tangapulj."
S.PHON: "kəʎu, səɭapaj aŋa mən; uri maquaŋ amən tusa taŋapuʎ."
S.TRANSL: “Come; we’re in trouble. We’re going to be wiped out by this lion”

S.FORM: vaik a sema zua tiamadju a maḍusa, kematsu tua vakela.
S.PHON: vaik a səma zua tiamaɉu a maɖusa, kəmaʦu tua vakəɭa.
S.FORM: vaik a sema zua tiamadju a madrusa, kemacu tua vakela.
S.PHON: vaik a səma zua tiamaɉu a maɖusa, kəmaʦu tua vakəɭa.
S.TRANSL: So the two went there, carrying (bows and) arrows.

S.FORM: "nu kemudakuda qadaw mangtjez a keman?"
S.PHON: "nu kəmudakuda qadaw maŋcəz a kəman"
S.FORM: "nu kemudakuda qadaw mangtjez a keman?"
S.PHON: "nu kəmudakuda qadaw maŋʦəz a kəman"
S.TRANSL: “At what time of day does it come to eat people?”

S.FORM: "nu i vavaw tsinedasan mangtjez."
S.PHON: "nu i vavaw ʦinədasan maŋcəz."
S.FORM: "nu i vavaw cinedasan mangtjez."
S.PHON: "nu i vavaw ʦinədasan maŋʦəz."
S.TRANSL: “It comes when the sun is above its rising place.”

S.FORM: kemaɫava tiamadju.
S.PHON: kəmaʎava tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: kemaljava tiamadju.
S.PHON: kəmaʎava tiamaɉu.

S.FORM: ka mangtjez a tangapuɫ, panaqen sa papatsayin; saka maleva aravats tiamadju a Vutsuɫ.
S.PHON: ka maŋcəz a taŋapuʎ, panaqən sa papaʦajin; saka maɭəva aravaʦ tiamaɉu a vuʦuʎ.
S.FORM: ka mangtjez a tangapulj, panaqen sa papacayin; saka maleva aravac tiamadju a Vuculj.
S.PHON: ka maŋʦəz a taŋapuʎ, panaqən sa papaʦajin; saka maɭəva aravaʦ tiamaɉu a vuʦuʎ.
S.TRANSL: When the lion came, they shot and killed it. The Vutsuɫ people were very glad.

S.FORM: "uɫa timun anga kikazeɫuzeɫu tua imaza penuɫat."
S.PHON: "uʎa timun aŋa kikazəʎuzəʎu tua imaza pənuʎat."
S.FORM: "ulja timun anga kikazeljuzelju tua imaza penuljat."
S.PHON: "uʎa timun aŋa kikazəʎuzəʎu tua imaza pənuʎat."
S.TRANSL: “All the tribute from here can go to you.”

S.FORM: qau vaik tiamadju a tsemikel a sema Tjuvasavasai.
S.PHON: qau vaik tiamaɉu a ʦəmikəɭ a səma cuvasavasai.
S.FORM: qau vaik tiamadju a cemikel a sema Tjuvasavasai.
S.PHON: qau vaik tiamaɉu a ʦəmikəɭ a səma ʦuvasavasai.

S.FORM: qau ti Ɫautu anga kisan qempuqepu tua kazeɫu i Vutsuɫ.
S.PHON: qau ti ʎautu aŋa kisan qəmpuqəpu tua kazəʎu i vuʦuʎ.
S.FORM: qau ti Ljautu anga kisan qempuqepu tua kazelju i Vuculj.
S.PHON: qau ti ʎautu aŋa kisan qəmpuqəpu tua kazəʎu i vuʦuʎ.
S.TRANSL: Ɫautu became the tribute collector among the Vutsuɫ.

S.FORM: nu patsatsikel tiamadju a pasa Vutsuɫ, kitsuɫu a sema zua tua qinaɫan anga tiamadju a maḍusa; mana palaingan nua naqaɫangan.
S.PHON: nu paʦaʦikəɭ tiamaɉu a pasa vuʦuʎ, kiʦuʎu a səma zua tua qinaʎan aŋa tiamaɉu a maɖusa; mana paɭaiŋan nua naqaʎaŋan.
S.FORM: nu pacacikel tiamadju a pasa Vuculj, kiculju a sema zua tua qinaljan anga tiamadju a madrusa; mana palaingan nua naqaljangan.
S.PHON: nu paʦaʦikəɭ tiamaɉu a pasa vuʦuʎ, kiʦuʎu a səma zua tua qinaʎan aŋa tiamaɉu a maɖusa; mana paɭaiŋan nua naqaʎaŋan.
S.TRANSL: When the two travelled through there, feasts were given for them in every village, and they were the leaders of all the villages.
